name: Vince Mendoza
abstract: Vince Mendoza (born November 17, 1961) is an American composer, music arranger and conductor. He debuted as a solo artist in 1989, and is known for his work conducting the Metropole Orkest and WDR Big Band Köln, as well as arranging music for musicians such as John Scofield, Joni Mitchell, Michael Brecker and Björk.

about_quotes.speaker: Michael Abene
about_quotes.evidence: But we had guys like Mintzer, myself, Grusin, Vince Mendoza did something, Tom Scott and Russell Farante. So it was everybody connected with GRP basically
about_quotes.doc_title: Michael Abene interviewed by Monk Rowe, Toronto, Canada, January 11, 2003

about_quotes.speaker: Tom Lellis
about_quotes.evidence: I spoke to Mike Abene and Vince Mendoza and Chuck Owen... I spoke and talked at length with three of the arrangers out of the four.
about_quotes.doc_title: Tom Lellis interviewed by Monk Rowe, Clinton, New York, July 31, 2007

about_quotes.speaker: Rick Margitza
about_quotes.evidence: I was reading an article, an interview of Vince Mendoza and he was talking about two or three minutes of music
about_quotes.doc_title: Rick Margitza interviewed by Monk Rowe, via Zoom, November 22, 2024

about_quotes.speaker: Karolina Strassmayer
about_quotes.evidence: A couple of weeks ago we just did a program with Vince Mendoza — he arranged Michel LeGrand’s music.
about_quotes.doc_title: Karolina Strassmayer interviewed by Monk Rowe, May 25, 2023

about_quotes.speaker: Konitz Lee
about_quotes.evidence: Vince Mendoza is the man in charge now. He’s a fine writer also.
